framework.name: MCP Security Theater Hypothesis
framework.description: Argues that MCP's static protocol-based security model creates false confidence while failing to address real agentic risks: contextual decision-making, tool chaining, emergent behavior, and indirect data exfiltration. Claims MCP 'secures the connection but not the consequence.'
framework.raw_quote: MCP secures the connection, but not the consequence. Just because an agent is MCP-compliant doesn't mean it's behaving safely.
